title: Morning FX & Macro Review — 12 May 2026
headline: Dollar firmer into US CPI; FOMC minutes lean patient; PBoC fixes test the topside
executive_summary:
CPI is the binary. Everything else today conditions on it.

Asia traded as a quiet continuation of the post-NFP US-dollar rebuild. European futures call flat with a defensive tilt. The three swing variables — US CPI, the 10-year supply, and the FOMC minutes — are clustered into the US afternoon.
fx_commentary: The dollar enters today with a modest long skew, broadly built in the post-NFP session. Cross-rates are quiet; the day's catalyst defines the direction. Specific levels are deliberately not quoted in this template.
rates_commentary: Front-end USD rates are the cleanest expression of today's binary. The 5s30s curve has flattened modestly on the session; we hold the steepener into the long-end supply with a stop on Friday's close.
equities_commentary: Risk assets open with a defensive tilt. Equity sensitivity to rates is in its bottom-quintile percentile on ERP — any further front-end re-pricing higher tests duration tolerance across the index.
commodities_commentary: Energy is bid on a normalised geopolitical premium; backwardation is intact on the front of the Brent curve. Metals are quiet, with the China-stimulus narrative providing a soft floor for copper.

risk_themes: US CPI binary — base case 3.1% headline / +0.3% core
ECB Schnabel QT framing — hawkish risk

strategist_view.headline: One binary, three windows
strategist_view.body: Today reduces to a single conditional and three observation windows around it. The cleanest expression is in the front-end of the USD curve rather than in spot FX, and the cleanest hedge is against the asymmetric tail rather than the base case. Discipline on size matters more than directional conviction.

macro_overview.opening: The week's macro grid resolves into a single conditional print and a set of confirming auctions and central-bank commentary. We approach with the dollar moderately bid into the data and the curve modestly bear-flattened versus a fortnight ago.
macro_overview.whats_moving: Two flows did the work overnight. Real-money sellers in the dollar funders on the post-NFP momentum trade, and leveraged accounts paying topside vol on the yen as the street rebuilt convex hedges against a soft-CPI reversal.
macro_overview.rates_view: The front end is the cleanest read on today's catalyst — historically rich versus the year-end OIS strip on a six-month lookback. The 5s30s steepener carries positively into the long-end auction; we hold it with a stop on Friday's close.
macro_overview.cross_asset_thesis: The dollar is the second-order driver for risk today; the primary driver remains the front-end re-pricing. Equity sensitivity to rates is in its bottom-quintile percentile by ERP — any further front-end re-pricing higher tests duration tolerance across the complex.

what_changed.summary: Versus a fortnight ago, the front-end curve has re-priced more hawkish, the dollar has firmed against the funders, and FX vol skew has rebuilt yen-call demand. Specific deltas not shown — Live market levels unavailable at generation time.
deltas: Front-end USD rates: re-priced more hawkish on a 2-week basis
Dollar: firmer vs G10 funders, range-bound vs commodity bloc
FX vol: 1m EUR/USD skew rebuilt EUR-put bid
FX vol: 1m USD/JPY skew flipped JPY-call bid for the first time since June
Fixed-income vol: MOVE index elevated into the catalyst window

key_takeaways.text: CPI is the binary — base case 3.1% headline / +0.3% core. Anything else re-prices the curve.
Front-end USD rates are historically rich versus year-end OIS — cheap on a soft surprise.
Specific pivot and target levels not quoted — Live market levels unavailable at generation time.
FOMC minutes the secondary binary at 19:00 — patient language is consensus.
JPY-call skew rebuilt; street hedging soft-CPI USD reversal via topside.

trade_ideas.catalyst: US CPI 13:30 GMT + FOMC minutes 19:00 GMT
trade_ideas.rationale: Levered USD-long, real-money receiving belly. Path lower on soft print. Size 2/3 pre-data; cut on 1.0890 reclaim failure.
2y still 22bp above Dec-26 OIS. Soft CPI + stop-through auction = 8-12bp catch-up. Hedge tail via DXY topside calls.

trade_ideas.catalyst: Hot CPI or hawkish FOMC minutes
trade_ideas.rationale: RR flipped JPY-call bid. Spot drifted without topside cover. Convex tail cheap; carry-positive through 152.50.

trade_ideas.vehicle: Jun-26 Brent vs Dec-26 Brent calendar
trade_ideas.catalyst: OPEC+ extension + EIA draw + headline risk
trade_ideas.rationale: Spec longs cut 11k in 2w. Backwardation intact ($1.20 1m-6m). Headline premium leaked — fair-to-cheap. Asymmetric on geo noise.
SNB tolerance for CHF strength tested at 0.96. ECB minutes lean patient. Carry +80bp ann.; hawkish-CPI safe-haven flow is the stop.

session_breakdown.asia: A quiet continuation of the post-NFP dollar rebuild. Japan equities softer on the yen-cross reversal. China liquidity supportive.
session_breakdown.europe: Cash equity opens with a defensive tilt — utilities and staples outperforming cyclicals at the open. ZEW expectations and the ECB Schnabel panel are the European-session variables.
session_breakdown.us: Pre-CPI quiet. The catalyst landscape clusters: CPI at 13:30 GMT, the 10-year auction at 17:00, FOMC minutes at 19:00, and Williams the first scheduled Fed voice after the print.

cross_asset.title: Rates → FX
cross_asset.body: Front-end USD rates remain the cleanest driver for the dollar side of the trade; spot FX is a second-order expression.

cross_asset.title: FX → Equities
cross_asset.body: Yen-cross reversal continues to feed Japan equity weakness. For US equities, the dollar is a second-order driver; the primary driver is rates.

cross_asset.title: Commodities → FX
cross_asset.body: Brent backwardation provides a soft floor for the commodity bloc. The CAD cross watches the front of the Brent curve more than spot.

cross_asset.title: Rates → Equities
cross_asset.body: Equity ERP is in its bottom-quintile percentile by historical comparison — duration tolerance is the variable to watch on any further front-end re-pricing.

risk_warnings.title: CPI binary at 13:30 GMT
risk_warnings.body: Primary risk event of the week. Pre-position carefully: we are 2/3 size on short EUR/USD going in. Soft surprise (≤+0.2% core) is the asymmetric tail — dollar gives back the week's gains in an hour.

risk_warnings.title: USD/JPY 153.00 intervention zone
risk_warnings.body: Hot CPI lifts spot through 153.00 cleanly. MoF watching; verbal intervention risk before action. Manage long USD/JPY topside vol with awareness of the level.

risk_warnings.title: Treasury 10y auction tail risk
risk_warnings.body: Indirect bid has been weak since March. A tail >+1bp re-prices the long end 2-3bp and pressures the 5s30s steepener we hold.

consensus_calls.impact: September cut conviction the swing variable

geopolitical.narrative: Geopolitical risk premium has compressed across the major axes over the past week. US–China dialogue remains constructive at the margin; Middle East tail risk has been priced out of front-month Brent. The recent G7 communiqué reaffirmed coordination on tariff harmonisation.

regions.name: US – China
regions.headline: Trade dialogue constructive; tariff framework stable
regions.detail: Recent working-level talks have not produced a breakdown. Treasury Secretary remarks at the Bretton Woods anniversary panel are the next scheduled signal.

regions.name: Russia – Ukraine
regions.headline: Frontline static; secondary sanctions enforcement up
regions.detail: Frontline static; secondary-sanctions enforcement higher on the week. EU package adopted recently; commodity-flow disruption is priced into the front of the Brent calendar.

regions.name: Middle East
regions.headline: No new flashpoints; tanker traffic normalised
regions.detail: No new flashpoints. Tanker transit volumes have normalised. The Brent geopolitical premium has compressed materially from its March wides.

regions.name: Taiwan Strait
regions.headline: Routine PLA patrols; no new escalation
regions.detail: Routine PLA patrols; no incremental escalation. The watch is on the upcoming cabinet confirmations.

instruments_to_watch.why_today: The cleanest read-through from the US CPI binary. Positioning into the print has rebuilt USD-long from real-money flows; the pair sits inside its recent trading range.
instruments_to_watch.catalyst: US CPI · April · 13:30 GMT · FOMC minutes 19:00 GMT
instruments_to_watch.desk_focus: Watch the reaction asymmetry around the print: a soft surprise reverses the week's USD-long quickly. Specific price levels not shown — Live market levels unavailable at generation time.

instruments_to_watch.why_today: Front-end USD rates are the most direct expression of the CPI/FOMC binary. The 2Y has been trading above Dec-26 OIS for most of the past two weeks.
instruments_to_watch.catalyst: US CPI 13:30 GMT · 10Y auction 17:00 GMT · FOMC minutes 19:00 GMT
instruments_to_watch.desk_focus: Indirect bid into the 10Y supply is the auction-tail variable. Specific yield levels not shown — Live market levels unavailable at generation time.

instruments_to_watch.why_today: The 1m 25-delta risk reversal has rebuilt JPY-call bid for the first time since the BoJ June meeting, suggesting the street is hedging a soft-CPI USD reversal via topside.
instruments_to_watch.catalyst: US CPI · BoJ Ueda commentary later in the week
instruments_to_watch.desk_focus: Intervention-zone risk if the pair extends. MoF verbal-warning history kicks in above the recent range high.

instruments_to_watch.why_today: Specs have cut length over the past two weeks per CFTC. Front-month backwardation is intact and the geopolitical headline premium has compressed.
instruments_to_watch.catalyst: EIA inventories · OPEC+ commentary · MENA headline flow
instruments_to_watch.desk_focus: Asymmetry on any geopolitical re-escalation. Specific price and curve levels not shown — Live market levels unavailable at generation time.

instruments_to_watch.why_today: SNB tolerance for CHF strength is being tested at the lower end of the recent range. ECB minutes lean patient and Schnabel's QT framing today is the catalyst on the EUR leg.
instruments_to_watch.catalyst: ECB Schnabel 10:00 GMT · SNB Jordan later in the week
instruments_to_watch.desk_focus: Verbal-intervention risk from the SNB if the pair extends lower. Specific levels not shown — Live market levels unavailable at generation time.
